mainHeading: GST Returns Filing Online – Expert GST Return Filing Service

introduction.heading: Introduction and Its Compliance
body: GST Returns are filed by each registered dealer on the GSTN portal, containing details of the sales, purchases, input tax credit (ITC) and output tax liability. Filing of GST returns is compulsory under the Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017 for all registered dealers, be it a person, company, LLP or any other kind of business entity. The rate of remittance is depending on the nature of business and type of scheme selected (regular, composition, etc.). Professionals Law provide holistic support for receiving timely and precise service for all GST returns (GSTR-1, GSTR-3B, GSTR-4, etc.) so as to be fully compliant with the same and minimize interest/penalties and avoid suspension or revocation of registration under GST.

whyNeeded.heading: Why It Is Needed
body: It is mandatory for all entities that are registered under the GST Law to file returns. If the form is not filed or not filed in time, it may result in penalties, interest or cancellation of the GSTIN. It is mandatory if you want to claim ITC, stay compliant and establish a good business credit. GST returns offer a very clear audit trail for the government. Professionals Law makes all your GST declarations accurately in time, enabling your business to stay clear of those ridiculous penalties and remain a tax compliant organization.

body.title: Avoid Penalties and Interest
body.description: Timely return filing helps businesses steer clear of late fees, interest and compliance notices from the GST department.

body.title: Claim Input Tax Credit (ITC)
body.description: Proper return filing allows businesses to avail eligible input tax credits, thereby reducing their overall tax liability.

body.title: Business Credibility
body.description: Regular GST return filing boosts your company’s credibility with vendors, customers, financial institutions and the government.

body.title: Smooth Compliance
body.description: Filing ensures a transparent and updated tax record, avoiding scrutiny and audits by tax authorities.

body.title: Eligibility for Loans and Tenders
body.description: GST-compliant businesses are preferred by banks and institutions for credit, funding and government tenders.

criteria: All businesses and individuals registered under GST
Businesses with an annual turnover exceeding the prescribed
Entities involved in inter-state supplies, e-commerce or input service distribution
Casual taxable persons and non-resident taxable persons

documents: Sales and purchase invoices for the applicable tax period
Details of outward supplies (GSTR-1) and inward supplies (GSTR-2A/2B)
DSC or EVC credentials for filing

body.description: Professionals Law gathers and reviews sales/purchase data.
Reconciliation of GSTR-2A/2B with purchase records.
Preparation of the applicable return form (e.g., GSTR-1, GSTR-3B, etc.).
Returns are reviewed and approved by the client.
Professionals Law files returns using DSC/EVC.
Filing acknowledgment is shared with the client for records.
Professionals Law provides reminders and support for future return deadlines.

body.title: Late Fees
body.description: ₹50 per day (₹25 CGST + ₹25 SGST), subject to a maximum cap.

body.title: Interest
body.description: 18% per annum on outstanding tax liability.

body.title: Suspension/Cancellation
body.description: Continuous non-filing may lead to cancellation of GSTIN.

body.title: Blocking of E-way Bill Generation
body.description: Non-compliant GSTINs may be restricted from generating e-way bills.

body.title: GSTR-1
body.description: Monthly/Quarterly - 11th of next month (Monthly) / 13th of next month (Quarterly)

body.title: GSTR-3B
body.description: Monthly - 20th of next month

body.title: GSTR-4
body.description: Quarterly (Composition Scheme) - 18th of month after quarter ends

body.title: GSTR-9
body.description: Annual - 31st December of the next financial year

body.question: What is a GST return?
body.answer: A GST return is a form that contains details of income, expenses, tax collected and tax paid, filed with the GST department.

body.question: Who is required to file GST returns?
body.answer: Every GST-registered person, including businesses, freelancers and e-commerce sellers.

body.question: What are the consequences of not filing GST returns?
body.answer: Late filing attracts penalties, interest and may lead to cancellation of GST registration.

body.question: What are the different types of GST returns?
body.answer: GSTR-1, GSTR-3B, GSTR-4, GSTR-9, GSTR-9C, etc., based on nature and size of business.

body.question: Can GST returns be revised once filed?
body.answer: No, currently GST returns once filed cannot be revised. Amendments can be made in the subsequent period.

body.question: What is the difference between GSTR-1 and GSTR-3B?
body.answer: GSTR-1 is for reporting outward supplies, while GSTR-3B is a summary return including ITC and tax payment.

body.question: Is return filing mandatory even with no sales?
body.answer: Yes, NIL returns must be filed to maintain compliance.

body.question: Does Professionals Law file GST returns monthly or quarterly?
body.answer: Professionals Law handles both monthly and quarterly filings based on your registration type.

body.question: Can Professionals Law handle GST compliance for multi-state operations?
body.answer: Yes, Professionals Law supports GST compliance across India for businesses with multiple state registrations.
